text:
In the quiet town nestling between autumn leaves and winter frost, there was a wise old owl. Each dawn, she'd listen attentively to the whispers of the forest inhabitants. Among them was a peculiar creature—an orphaned squirrel who spoke owl.

One autumn day, the squirrel bore eyes filled with dreams.

"The trees sing your favorite tune."

"And the stars listen."

"But I wish."

"The stars crave stories."

So she spun tales into existence. With each nighttime flight, her tales grew broader.

A hero once sailed across skies so vast;
A wise old owl lost beneath velvet pads.

An owl who loved cats, flew through dusky skies.

These words became birds that soared.

Each version of herself became legendary.

One star blinked just like your eyes.

That night, dreams came true.

But autumn winds carried truths too.

No one can wish forever.

That star blinked out.

Every bedtime story lost its shine.

Yet the squirrel slept soundly.

For dreams change seasons.

To wishes beyond cold
